ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2026 Continued To Jun-16-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned $400.00 for failure to appear. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to June 16,2026, at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-29-2026 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-07-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2025 Continued To Feb-25-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT 300 F. OGAWA PLAZA LP, RUBICON POINT PARTNERS MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

is granted. All claims alleged against the moving defendants arise from their protected petitioning activity in the unlawful detainer action and prior civil lawsuit. Plaintiffs have failed to present any admissible evidence to show that their claims against the moving defendants have minimal merit. Moreover, those claims are barred by the litigation privilege. As prevailing parties on this motion, moving defendants are awarded reasonable attorneys' fees of $14,077.50. The request for fees for filing the reply papers is denied because it was not reasonably necessary to file reply papers in light of the patently deficient opposition papers.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2025 Continued To Nov-25-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT 300 F. OGAWA PLAZA LP, RUBICON POINT PARTNERS MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

is continued to November 25, 2025. No later than November 17,2025 by 5:00 p.m. parties to submit supplemental briefs (not to exceed 2 pages) addressing whether the venue is proper in San Francisco. The allegations of the Complaint in paragraph 6 do not support the choice of venue.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: K.Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
